All sub-tasks complete. cosign-signing-key-images (image signing) and cosign-signing-key-artifacts (artifact signing) are isolated. forgejo-mcp PR #164 merged 2026-05-26. Old cosign-signing-key still present pending final removal after ArgoCD sync confirms both new Secrets are live.

Decision recorded: use helm.values (YAML string) for any override that needs null/empty to delete a chart default. valuesObject strips JSON nulls at apiserver storage. Fix already in commit 5dfbeab. No further action needed.

Decision recorded: always identify which ArgoCD instance owns the Application via metadata.namespace before editing AppProject. Two AppProjects named 'op1st' exist — openshift-gitops/projects/op1st.yaml (cluster-mgmt) vs op1st-gitops/projects/op1st.yaml (workload). Name is not unique; namespace is the discriminator. Operational knowledge only, no code change needed.

Decision: keep strict sourceRepos allowlist (status quo). Rationale: sourceRepos=['*'] is a supply-chain regression — any repo can source manifests if an attacker creates an in-scope Application; namespace restriction does not compensate. The per-PR maintenance burden of adding a new repo IS the security gate, not a problem. Maintenance discipline: add repo to allowlist in same PR as the Application that references it. Both AppProjects (openshift-gitops and op1st-gitops) follow this pattern correctly.
cosign-signing-key deleted from repo. Both cosign-signing-key-artifacts and cosign-signing-key-images confirmed live in op1st-pipelines via kubernetes MCP. ArgoCD will prune old Secret on next sync.
